page: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-416 is an x86_64 architecture server within the memory-optimized m2 family. It features 416 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon processors running at 2.7 GHz, mapped to 208 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 11,776.0 GB, providing 28.31 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 caches. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier multi-core performance, as demonstrated by its stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 353,160.93 and a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 101,658.81. Memory bandwidth is highly optimized, reaching 20,571.16 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 658.18 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,004.0 and a weak stress-ng single-core rating. Additionally, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, and text generation for small LLM models is limited to 13.9 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a highly specialized, high-density resource class designed to consolidate massive datasets that would otherwise require complex distributed clustering. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to provision external block storage as needed. The m2-ultramem-416 is best suited for massive in-memory databases, such as SAP HANA or large-scale Redis deployments, where its top-tier memory bandwidth and high multi-core throughput can be fully leveraged.
description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-416 is a highly specialized memory-optimized server designed for massive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 416 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (208 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz and an extraordinary 11,776.0 GB of RAM, yielding 28.31 GB per core. The server lacks bundled local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU capabilities and exceptional memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 20,571.16 GB/sec and Redis SET operations exceeding 324 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and text-generation speeds are weak. This profile makes the server highly cost-efficient for consolidating massive in-memory databases and enterprise applications, though it is less suited for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-416 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 11.5 TB of RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for massive in-memory databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m2-ultramem-416 is a memory-optimized server featuring 416 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 11.5 TB RAM, built for massive in-memory databases.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized server delivering eleven terabytes of RAM and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise in-memory databases.
bullet_points: 416 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 11,776 GB of RAM.
Top-tier memory bandwidth of up to 20,571 GB/sec.
Excellent multi-core performance and high-throughput Redis operations.
No bundled local storage or integrated GPUs.
Weak single-core processing and poor text-generation speeds.
Optimized for massive in-memory databases and enterprise consolidation.
